text:
First, let's calculate how many apples Walter ate. Walter bought 60 apples and ate 2/5 of them.

\[ 2/5 \times 60 = 24 \]

So, Walter ate 24 apples. That means he had

\[ 60 - 24 = 36 \]

apples remaining.

Next, Walter gave 25% of these remaining apples to his sister. To find out how many that is, we calculate 25% of 36.

\[ 25\% \text{ of } 36 = 0.25 \times 36 = 9 \]

So, he gave 9 apples to his sister. That means he had

\[ 36 - 9 = 27 \]

apples left.

Walter then sold the remaining apples at $3 each. So, to find out how much money he received from selling the apples, we multiply the number of apples he sold by the price per apple.

\[ 27 \times 3 = 81 \]

Thus, Walter received $81 from selling the apples to his uncle.
